p.seg: Earl Waldef sends greeting to all sworn men and friends, French and English.
Let those to come as well as those present know

me to have given, and made firm by this my charter, with the agreement of my heir, to God and St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, common pasture upon Lammermuir, with me and my men, by these boundaries, that is: from Baldred’s Stane by the highway as far as the Hazliecleugh and thence downwards as the Hazliecleugh falls into the Bothwell and thence as far as the boundaries of Innerwick; for the salvation of my soul and my ancestors’ and successors’; in everlasting alms, as freely and quitely and honourably from all secular services and customs as any alms is freely, quitely and honourably given or held. And if they shall quit the pasture of Innerwick, their sheep-folds shall follow their flocks, with their bothies that belong to the said sheep-folds within the marches named before, without manual labour, and with my own bothies at Belton.
This donation I and my heirs shall warrant to the foresaid monks against all men for ever.

With these witnesses, Aelina, countess; Ralph, chaplain; John, chaplain; Walter, chaplain; Hugh of Duns; Patrick the earl’s brother; Edward the earl’s uncle; Gilbert Fraser; Adam son of Aldan; Aldan the seneschal; Gamel of Pitcox; Adam of Fogo; Gilbert son of Walter; Stephen Papedy; Warin le Malle; Adam son of Edgar; Adam of Edington.
